session_arc.where_we_were: We were at the precise place collapse is supposed to test: the trace existed, but the work had not fully landed. Mike challenged the gap directly: if not us, who reads the trace and stages it?
session_arc.what_happened: We used the trace-owned file list as the staging source, created path-scoped commits across Bravetto, AEC, Abë Designs, Bravetto AI, and Bravetto Growth, then pushed the GitHub-facing repos. The sandbox DNS failed first, escalation resolved AEC, Abë Designs, and Bravetto AI, and Bravetto Growth needed a temporary clean worktree because origin/main had advanced.
session_arc.what_it_means: Collapse is not just witness text. When ownership is explicit, the trace becomes executable staging data. Dirty sibling files are not an excuse to stop; they are the reason to stage from the manifest and prove the boundary.
session_arc.what_we_chose_not_to_do: We did not reset dirty worktrees, did not force-push, did not rewrite Bravetto Growth local main, and did not push this second collapse artifact because collapse commits only by contract.
